info.title: Azure Media Services Asset Migration Tool
info.description: A command line tool to migrate your media assets from Azure Media Services.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description:
Analyze assets for migration and generate report.
Example(s):
amsmigrate analyze -s <subscriptionid> -g <resourcegroup> -n <account>
This will analyze the given media account and produce a summary report.

options.name: --batch-size
options.description: Batch size for parallel processing. [default: 1]

options.name: --analysis-type
options.description:
The kind of analysis to do.
Summary - Summary of migration
Detailed - A detailed classification of assets,
Report - A migration report [default: Summary]

options.name: --log-level
options.description: The log level for logging [default: Warning]

options.name: --log-directory
options.description: The directory where the logs are written. Defaults to the temporary directory [default: /tmp/]

options.name: --subscription
options.description: The azure subscription to use

options.name: --resource-group
options.description: The resource group of the account beging migrated

options.name: --account-name
options.description: The target Azure Media Services or Azure Storage Account being migrated.

options.name: --cloud-type
options.description:
The destination cloud you are migrating to.
For Azure refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/azure.identity.defaultazurecredential?view=azure-dotnet
For AWS refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sdk-for-net/v3/developer-guide/creds-locate.html
For GCP refer to https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/application-default-credentials
Depending on the type of authentcation you may have to set some environment variables. [default: Azure]

options.name: --daemon-mode
options.description: Run in daemon mode and do not use ASCII graphics. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --created-after
options.description: Query entities created after given date.

options.name: --created-before
options.description: Query entities created before given date.

options.name: --filter
options.description:
An ODATA condition to filter the resources.
e.g.: "name eq 'asset1'" to match an asset with name 'asset1'.
Visit https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/media-services/latest/filter-order-page-entities-how-to for more information.

options.name: --mark-complete
options.description: Mark completed assets by writing metadata on the container [default: True]

options.name: --skip-migrated
options.description: Skip assets that have been migrated already. [default: True]

options.name: --packager
options.description: The packager to use. [default: Shaka]

options.name: --working-dir
options.description: The working directory to use migration. [default: /tmp/AMSMigrate]

options.name: --copy-nonstremable
options.description: Copy non-stremable assets (Assets without .ism file) as is. [default: True]

options.name: --batch-size
options.description: Batch size for parallel processing. [default: 1]

options.name: --storage-path
options.description:
The output storage account or path to upload the migrated assets.
This is specific to the cloud you are migrating to.
e.g: For Azure specify the storage account name or the URL <https://accountname.blob.core.windows.net>

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Overwrite the files in the destination. [default: False]

options.name: --path-template
options.description:
Path template to determine the final path in the storage where files are uploaded.
Can use ${AssetName} ${AssetId} ${ContainerName} or ${LocatorId}.
e.g., videos/${AssetName} will upload to a container named 'videos' with path begining with the asset name. [default: ${AssetId}/]

options.name: --asset-names
options.description: Select specific asset(s) by name. You can specify multiple assets

options.name: --log-level
options.description: The log level for logging [default: Warning]

options.name: --log-directory
options.description: The directory where the logs are written. Defaults to the temporary directory [default: /tmp/]

options.description: The azure subscription to use
